purpose: Help the PPT AI Agent start from a branded canvas instead of a finished blueprint, then compose every final slide from template/components based on the page plan.

blueprints.role: Directory of copyable canvas TSX blueprint pages.
rules: Do not treat the canvas files as finished slide structures.
Copy or derive the selected canvas blueprint into ./slides before adapting it for the concrete deck.
Use the canvas page only as a branded frame before composing real content.
Read ./components/README.md before editing any final slide.

slides.role: Directory of final slide pages for the concrete deck.
rules: manifest.json should only point to TSX files in this directory.
AI must replace canvas slot guides with component compositions tailored to the page title, outline, audience, and grounded content.
Slides here should not remain unchanged canvas copies in a final deck.
Choose component families from page semantics, not from visual convenience.
Prefer composing existing components before adding new components.

data.role: Optional content data for concrete decks.
rules: JSON should carry content only, not page-structure decisions.
If layout or component choice is wrong, edit slides/*.tsx.

agent_workflow_notes: This template group intentionally provides canvas-level starting points, not finished page designs.
Before editing slides/*.tsx, read components/README.md and identify the component families that fit the page plan.
Use CoverCanvas for only the opening page, SectionFocusCanvas for section transitions, and ContentCanvas for ordinary content pages.
Replace slot guides with concrete component composition; do not leave guide labels in the final deck.
If a page resembles a finished blueprint page too closely, restructure it by composing lower-level components instead.
